name: Disney® Premier Visa® Card
effective_cost: Low, but only if you're already spending at Disney

verdict.text: Situational — only a keep if you regularly book Disney trips, stream Disney+/Hulu/ESPN+, or shop at Disney parks

earn_rates.category: Gas stations & most Disney U.S. locations

earn_note: No spend caps on any tier, which is unusual for a sub-$50 co-brand card. But the real catch isn't a subscription requirement — it's that every dollar earned becomes Disney Rewards Dollars, a currency with almost no use outside Disney. Don't get this card for the earn rate; get it for the park perks and Disney-locked spend.

redemption_options.method: Disney parks, resorts, cruises, shopping & dining (or a statement credit toward airfare via Pay Yourself Back)

points.note: Disney Rewards Dollars are dollar-denominated and always worth 1¢ — there's no bonus redemption to chase. The catch is where you can spend them: Disney parks, resorts, cruises, shopping, dining, and (via Pay Yourself Back) airline statement credits. No cash-out option and no transfer partners.

transfer_partners.highlight: Disney Rewards Dollars aren't a transferable currency — they're locked to Disney spend (or, via Pay Yourself Back, a statement credit toward airfare). No airline or hotel transfer partners.

credits.name: DoorDash DashPass
credits.subtitle: 1 year free · then auto-renews paid
credits.description: Complimentary 12-month DashPass membership ($0 delivery fees, lower service fees), plus up to $10 off per quarter on non-restaurant orders through Dec 2027.
tips: Activate it — otherwise it's pure unused value.
After 12 months it auto-renews at DashPass's then-current monthly rate unless you cancel. Set a reminder.

protection_note: A retail card's protection suite, not a travel card's — purchase protection, extended warranty, and baggage delay are here as standard Visa Signature benefits, but there's no trip cancellation/interruption, no emergency medical, and no cell phone protection.
rental_note: Secondary auto rental collision damage waiver (standard Visa Signature benefit) — decline the counter's CDW and pay with this card. Excludes exotic/luxury makes and rentals over 31 days; pays out after your personal auto policy.

services.name: Character photo experiences
services.detail: Cardmember-exclusive photo ops with a Disney PhotoPass photographer and complimentary digital downloads, at designated locations in Walt Disney World and Disneyland (up to 6 guests per cardmember, once per day).

services.name: Merchandise & dining discounts
services.detail: 10% off select merchandise at Disney Store, DisneyStore.com, and select in-park locations; 10% off select dining most days; 15% off select guided tours.

services.name: 0% APR on Disney vacation packages
services.detail: 6-month 0% promotional APR on qualifying Disney vacation package purchases, reverting to the standard variable APR (18.24%–29.99%) after the promo period.

services.name: Foreign transaction fee applies
services.detail: 3% on purchases made outside the US — not built for international use, including at Disneyland Paris.

additional_cards.title: Additional cards

benefits.text: Earns Disney Rewards Dollars on their spend (into your account)
Same purchase protection as the primary card

additional_cards.note: Additional cards are free — no reason not to add a trusted household member, especially if they'll be doing the theme-park spending.

timeline.text: Chase & Disney launched the Disney® Inspire Visa® Card ($149 AF) above Premier, aimed at frequent park/cruise visitors. Premier now sits as the mid-tier option between the no-fee Disney Visa and Inspire.

Welcome offer restructured from a $400 statement credit (after $1,000 spend) to a $200 Disney Gift Card eGift on approval plus a $100 statement credit after $500 spend — same $300 total, lower spend requirement, but split into a gift card instead of a full statement credit.
